name: Barley

pros.title: exceptional beta-glucan fibre case
pros.explanation: Barley has one of the strongest grain fibre stories, not just a decent one.

pros.title: elite carb quality for a grain
pros.explanation: The fibre plus very low GI combination is unusually strong for the category.

pros.title: strong whole-grain staple case
pros.explanation: It is one of the clearest examples of a grain that actually earns its category reputation.

cons.title: not suitable for people avoiding gluten
cons.explanation: That limits who can use it, even if the food itself is strong in-category.

cons.title: less convenient than simpler staples
cons.explanation: It asks a bit more effort and intention than some grains.

cons.title: less common default choice for most people
cons.explanation: Strong nutritionally, but not the easiest mainstream staple.

sourceNotes: Benchmark anchor for an obviously elite grain case.
Used to test whether the grain ruleset has enough ceiling for S-tier outcomes.
